title: Right-to-repair primer (find manuals/parts; rights overview) — informational, not legal advice
context: Many people do not know how to find repair manuals, spare parts, or serial/model information for their devices, nor are they aware of applicable right-to-repair legal frameworks. This primer provides a neutral, product-brand-neutral on-ramp: how to find legitimate manuals and parts sources, and a high-level overview of repair rights frameworks (EU, UK, US). It is strictly informational — not legal advice — and must carry an explicit 'informational, not legal advice' disclaimer. It must not reproduce copyrighted manuals and must not recommend specific products or brands.
objective: Author a neutral, sourced right-to-repair primer covering: how to find manuals and spare parts (legitimate sources); a high-level overview of repair-rights frameworks (EU/UK/US); framed as informational only with a prominent 'not legal advice' disclaimer; product/brand-neutral; reviewed for accuracy and neutrality.
acceptanceCriteria: The primer covers: how to find repair manuals (legitimate sources); how to find spare parts; serial/model number identification; a high-level, jurisdiction-aware overview of repair-rights frameworks (EU right-to-repair directive, UK, US state laws) citing authoritative sources
A prominent 'This primer is informational only and is not a substitute for legal advice. For advice specific to your situation, consult a qualified legal professional.' disclaimer is present
The primer does not reproduce copyrighted manuals; links to legitimate manual/parts sources only; is product- and brand-neutral with no affiliate links
Every factual claim cites an authoritative, reuse-permitted source with retrievedDate, sourceLicense, and reusePermitted; no verbatim copying of copyrighted text
The primer is reviewed for neutrality and accuracy by an approver distinct from the author; a PR-tied review-log entry is written
Content is published under CC-BY-4.0; renders from the data-002 schema; passes the CI content-governance gate
output: A committed, schema-valid right-to-repair primer (CC-BY-4.0) with the 'not legal advice' disclaimer, neutral and sourced content, and a PR-tied review-log entry showing distinct author and approver.
